consolidation_candidates.title: Unify Catalog Administration UI
consolidation_candidates.description: src/Web/Pages/Admin (Razor Pages) and src/BlazorAdmin/Pages/CatalogItemPage (Blazor) both implement catalog item administration. Forward engineering should consolidate this into a single Admin module/owner.

evidence.reason: Razor Pages admin catalog edit surface (ARCH-VIOL-002)
Blazor admin catalog edit surface for the same entity (ARCH-VIOL-002)

consolidation_candidates.recommendation: Do not consolidate code until the team confirms which surface (Razor Pages Admin vs BlazorAdmin) is canonical. Treat as a review item, not an automatic merge.

consolidation_candidates.title: Preserve ApplicationCore as a Single Domain/Application Module
consolidation_candidates.description: Basket, Order, Buyer, and Catalog entities, specifications, and application services already live in one cohesive ApplicationCore project. This module is a strong candidate to remain a single forward-engineering unit (or split internally along aggregate lines, see service-boundary-options.md) rather than being merged with Infrastructure or presentation projects.

current_modules.name: ApplicationCore
current_modules.module_ref: unknown (module ID not present in provided evidence; referenced by project name only)

consolidation_candidates.recommendation: No consolidation action required at this stage; flag as the likely 'core domain' anchor for future service boundary decisions.

consolidation_candidates.title: Review Contract Coupling Between PublicApi CatalogItemEndpoints and BlazorAdmin Catalog Services
consolidation_candidates.description: BlazorAdmin's CachedCatalogItemServiceDecorator wraps PublicApi's CatalogItemEndpoints request/response shapes directly. This is not a structural consolidation candidate, but a contract-ownership review candidate: either formalize this as a published API contract (preferred) or consider co-locating contract DTOs.

evidence.reason: Decorator service for catalog item operations in the admin frontend
Corresponding backend endpoint, likely consumed by the above decorator

consolidation_candidates.recommendation: Treat as a contract-formalization task (see api-contract-preservation-map.json), not a code consolidation.
